evidence.title: Relevant text extract from SDNY May 6, 2026 unsealing order
evidence.source_description: Text extracted from official SDNY PDF order in United States v. Tartaglione, Case No. 16-CR-832 (KMK), using web PDF text extraction during plaintiff merits investigation.
evidence.relevance: Primary court source proving the court granted unsealing of a note described in the order as an alleged suicide note purportedly authored by Jeffrey Epstein and directed the clerk to docket and unseal the material.

evidence.title: Guardian May 6, 2026 report on alleged Epstein suicide note
evidence.source_description: Accessible Guardian article text captured through web page extraction during plaintiff merits investigation.
evidence.relevance: Credible public reporting before the May 8 deadline that a federal judge unsealed an alleged suicide note written by Jeffrey Epstein, with note text, public availability timing, and adverse caveats on signature and verification.

evidence.title: Business Insider May 8, 2026 report on Mark Epstein forgery claim and DOJ/OIG absence
evidence.source_description: Business Insider article by Jacob Shamsian, page text retrieved with web open during defendant merits investigation. This is a concise relevant extract preserving metadata, adverse authenticity claims, and provenance/custody details.
evidence.relevance: Adverse source for authenticity and intent: before the May 8 deadline, Epstein's brother called the purported note a forgery; article also reports the note was not in DOJ Epstein files or the DOJ Inspector General report, and that it surfaced through Tartaglione's unrelated criminal case.

evidence.title: AP May 7, 2026 report on Epstein note and DOJ non-authentication caveat
evidence.source_description: Associated Press article metadata and body text extracted from captured AP HTML during plaintiff merits investigation.
evidence.relevance: Credible wire-service reporting that the note attributed to Epstein was public by May 7, 2026, and important adverse evidence that DOJ said it had not authenticated or previously seen the note.
